BANCO NACIONAL DE PANAMA — BNP PANAMA CANARY

Panama Risk Index (PARI)  |  Canal Chokepoint & Dollarized Economy Intelligence
WORLD'S #1 SHIPPING CHOKEPOINT  ·  FULLY DOLLARIZED  ·  COBRE PANAMA SHUT
Issued: 2026-07-06 PARI: 0.3850 WATCH Currency: USD (dollarized since 1904) Domain Slug: bnp_panama_canary Generated: 2026-07-06 04:36 UTC
WARNING — FULLY DOLLARIZED ECONOMY: Panama has operated on the US dollar since 1904. There is NO monetary policy lever available to the Banco Nacional de Panama. Every shock — canal disruption, mining shutdown, or external trade pressure — is a direct fiscal pass-through with zero central bank buffer.

Panama Key Facts

■ Panama Canal: 5% of global trade, 70M tonnes/year
■ Canal closure forces rerouting: +14,000 nm via Cape Horn
■ 2024 drought: ACP cut transits by 36%, costs +300%
■ Cobre Panama: $10B investment, 4% GDP, 75% exports — SHUT Nov 2023
■ Colon Free Trade Zone: World #2 after Hong Kong
■ Dollarized since 1904 — no monetary policy
KC_01 — SINGLE TRIGGER UNIQUE: If KC_01 (Panama Canal Closure) reaches a score of ≥0.85, the PARI zone is automatically forced to CRITICAL regardless of the weighted composite score. Current KC_01 = 0.4500 (threshold: 0.85). The canary is watching the canal.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3500  WATCH

Banco Nacional de Panamá operates a emerging, mid-tier supply chain network, with growing logistics infrastructure but limited redundancy — single-port dependencies and thin inventory buffers amplify chokepoint shocks. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Panama Canal (critical dependency, weight 0.99).

Critical import dependencies include crude oil, food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Panama is identified as a nearshoring beneficiary — attracting manufacturing FDI as global supply chains diversify away from China. This elevates near-term logistics complexity but structurally reduces long-run single-supplier dependency.

Primary exports — canal revenues, gold, bananas, shrimp — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3500 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Panama Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  FDI slowdown or political reversal on nearshoring → deferred logistics investment  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.1800  NORMAL

Banco Nacional de Panamá is classified as a emerging-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is commercial real estate (CRE) and office vacancy. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As an emerging market, Panama real estate is exposed to dual risks: domestic credit tightening and external capital outflows. Currency depreciation compounds mortgage stress for dollar-denominated property loans.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Panama are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.1800 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

Banco Nacional de Panamá faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Panama is shallow, with limited domestic capacity and heavy reliance on offshore reinsurance. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
